title: Rei Asuka Tragedy Mari
wikitext:
[]: (Rei + Asuka) - Tragedy = Mari?             Quote Post by EvaChero » Wed Jun 02, 2021 11:00 am It was just a thought that popped into my head after reading Kendrix' analysis of the Shinji/Rei relationship dynamics, but I wondered, being that you have the somewhat polar opposite personalities of Rei and Asuka and their personal tragedies [super run on sentence I know] does it seem rational that if you combined Rei and Asuka into one being...and removed the tragedies, would you then have all around middle ground perky Mari? Further could that be why they paired Shinji and Mari at the end? A sort of best of both worlds solution without the baggage? Just curious.

[]: Re: (Rei + Asuka) - Tragedy = Mari?             Quote Post by Blockio » Wed Jun 02, 2021 11:20 am I feel like this projection doesn't really do any of the characters justice. Sure, if you squint hard enough and only look at the most surface level character traits it kinda maps, but that is ignoring all the nuance that all three of them have

[]: Re: (Rei + Asuka) - Tragedy = Mari?             Quote Post by Kendrix » Wed Jun 02, 2021 12:40 pm I find this is super duper reductive to all the characters. If you look at the CR the impression I got that they were trying to come up with a character that would be equally different from both & add something truly new, which was hard because they were already contradictory - early on they explicitly threw out a lot of drafts where it seemed she had a role that could as well be given to one of the other three main girls. You could even say that the 'napping Asuka's job' drafts gave her a bit of Shinji's role - but all that was, at last, rejected until they settled on the particular Mari flavor/function, around the time Saakamoto was actually cast. There were also plans from the start to include some ship tease there which they didn't really get around to earlier; but as far as the actual ending goes it's at best suggested; No need to act like they were last seen in wedding clothes. Saying they "got paired" is rly overstating it but if you wanted to go with that read it would probably be in a function of representing change & support in general & something external to the old EVA as opposed to anything to do with the other three. If you look at statements by their VAs, Asuka is described as having a 'wall' in her heart, whilst Rei is said to have a deeply buried well - though the inner dynamic is very different, in both cases there's a disparity between the surface & the inside. Mari is completely different from both by going in the opposite extreme of having absolutely no filter, less than the average person. She blurts out whatever she thinks. If Rei & Asuka are two points at the end of a line, Mari is not the point in the middle; Rather, Asuka and Rei are the end points of a triangle. Since she ended up as a relatively static character for lack of time, the point of having her is mostly as a contrast figure.

[]: Re: (Rei + Asuka) - Tragedy = Mari?             Quote Post by EvaChero » Wed Jun 02, 2021 12:45 pm It's true, I'm speaking in only the most broad sense of character development. I really need to watch the rebuilds a few more times and maybe I'll pick up on the nuances better. I've started yet another re-watch of NGE on 'flix and I seem stuck in a rut as it were. I'll try to pay more attention to Mari, I've just yet to develop a real feel for her character that differentiates her from the other two as anything more than some kind of compromise? *Edit - oh and let me throw in that I'm pretty awful at reading people in real life so there's that. It probably makes me a little slow getting differences and motivations quickly.

[]: Re: (Rei + Asuka) - Tragedy = Mari?             Quote Post by Kendrix » Wed Jun 02, 2021 1:17 pm Perhaps the 'good faith analysis on Mari' essay is actually worth writing. In the meantime here's some salient CR snippets. "As for other images, there was the part of a Miko-san [shrine maiden] channeling the gods. She is not “practical” like Asuka, but thinks about “deep” things, like someone who somehow can see mysterious things like those connected with the gods. She is not “unworldly” to Rei’s extent and she talks a lot." "I gave O-sada [Yoshiyuki Sadamoto] and Makki a rough impression of her settei: her outward design was to be that of a English private school girl, a bit “O-nee-san”-like, maybe with a love of animals as well, and so on." [I saw her as] an “irresponsible” and “cunning” character. [To say] “cowardice” would be wrong; it was a “cunningness”108 at the opposite remove from “diligence”⁠, “obstinacy” [...] As a result, for me, Mari was a girl who made use of that “frivolousness” that was the opposite of diligence for her own benefit. There's also the bit from EVA chronicle that describes her as "born fighter, bottomless strenght keeps up her smile even at the worst moments, but has a cute side too" This is Saakamoto from the booklet interview: However, no matter what circumstances she was living under, Mari is fundamentally an optimist and does not worry too much. She may have her own worries, but she's not the type to show them, and she always seems to be having fun. I think that's what makes him such a contrast to Shinji and the other characters. She has no two faces, so I tried to play the role with as few complex nuances as possible. No matter how desperate the scene may seem, Mari believes that things are going in the right direction, and she is happy, which is the nuance of joy I tried to convey in my voice. I tried to keep her voice positive and not too sorrowful [...] feel that Mari really likes people. She likes Shinji, but I think Asuka is special to Mari. Even though Asuka treats her coldly, Mari always calls her "Princess" and respects and protects her as a special person But this is all about stuff that you wouldn't notice just from looking/ that wouldn't be obvious. If you wanna boil it down to the bare bones basics, - adrenaline junkie. Actually enjoys weird nightmarish things - eccentric - little sense of propriety. Sings, talks to herself, no personal space, sniffs ppl meoww... - accepting nice, supportive friend (you see this a bit in her dialogues with Shinji in Ha and Q, but it's not out in full force until the last movie with the Asuka interactions) - underneath that is old times stiff upper lip attitude, uses old military terms, fearless/serious - hobbies include animals, singing , reading & learning languages; messy quarters - mysterious - not in the sense that there's one big secret to find out (like, "Kaworu's an angel" or "Gendo's plan is to revive his wife" ), but that she doesn't talk much about herself or explain her actions - knows how the eva stuff works - does whatever the fuck she wants. Doesn't really join groups. No WILLE insignia, complains about having to work with Kaji etc; It's lampshaded that the blocked communications when stealing EVA 02, with EVA 05 she set the language to something ppl in the base can't understand... she just prefers to do her own thing unhampered, however, she does care about her comrades esp.. Asuka. If we look at what's deliberately pointed to as defining moments by the narrative where they have ppl deliberate react is Misato going "Whoever it is wants to do it alone" when she's hijacked EVA 02, and Kaji remarking on how she blew up EVA 5 without flinching.

[]: Re: (Rei + Asuka) - Tragedy = Mari?             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Wed Jun 02, 2021 2:37 pm Kendrix wrote: - mysterious - not in the sense that there's one big secret to find out (like, "Kaworu's an angel" or "Gendo's plan is to revive his wife" ), but that she doesn't talk much about herself or explain her actions

[]: Re: (Rei + Asuka) - Tragedy = Mari?             Quote Post by EvaChero » Wed Jun 02, 2021 2:48 pm @Kendrix, Thank You! that's fantastic! it's weird, I SEE some of these things like the whole "sniffing" people thing but take it in only on a purely surface level as just something the character does without being able to classify/quantify/apply it properly? It may be the same reason I miss most of that "fan service" stuff. I wish I had found this forum a year ago when I had bags of time for extra reading. It will sink in eventually. Funny, I've never had a problem understanding my dogs or maybe I'm quite the shallow human.

[]: Re: (Rei + Asuka) - Tragedy = Mari?             Quote Post by Slowpokeking » Wed Jun 02, 2021 6:23 pm She mostly took Mistao's elements. I think Mana=(Rei + Asuka) - Tragedy

[]: Re: (Rei + Asuka) - Tragedy = Mari?             Quote Post by Kendrix » Thu Jun 03, 2021 5:13 am Slowpokeking wrote: I think Mana=(Rei + Asuka) - Tragedy

[]: Re: (Rei + Asuka) - Tragedy = Mari?             Quote Post by Blockio » Fri Jun 04, 2021 1:05 pm Kendrix wrote: ...though maybe all these comparisons are because Rei and Asuka are so orthogonal that they form a basis in character space, such that every possible anime character can be expressed as a linear combination of x times Rei and y times Asuka.

[]: Re: (Rei + Asuka) - Tragedy = Mari?             Quote Post by Joseki » Mon Jun 07, 2021 12:11 am Eva main characters - what made them unique and relatable + relentless positivity = Mari

[]: Re: (Rei + Asuka) - Tragedy = Mari?             Quote Post by Zusuchan » Mon Jun 07, 2021 4:22 am Are you implying that Mari is not unique? An interesting thing to say about a relentlessly positive, seemingly eternally untroubled character. Certainly more unique than, say, Kensuke or Hikari, it'd seem. Over-all, I share the sentiment that trying to find out or develop Mari's character based around other Eva characters is a bit pointless-not because discussing her based on her differences and similarities and relevance to other characters is pointless, but because reducing her to other characters' qualities minus this plus that is pointless. Mari Makinami is not Asuka -+ blahblah or Misato -+ blahblah, she's Mari Makinami and treating her as that leads to far more interesting and worthwhile discussions on her, IMO. Edit: Forgot a "thing".

[]: Re: (Rei + Asuka) - Tragedy = Mari?             Quote Post by Kendrix » Mon Jun 07, 2021 8:35 am ^^^^^this

[]: Re: (Rei + Asuka) - Tragedy = Mari?             Quote Post by Blockio » Mon Jun 07, 2021 12:08 pm In quite agree.

[]: Re: (Rei + Asuka) - Tragedy = Mari?             Quote Post by EvaChero » Mon Jun 07, 2021 1:20 pm sorry geeks it really wasn't meant to be an insulting observation about Mari as a character, it was only a thought behind maybe why she was brought in in the first place so to speak. I'm not always great at getting thoughts to keys.

[]: Re: (Rei + Asuka) - Tragedy = Mari?             Quote Post by Zusuchan » Mon Jun 07, 2021 5:11 pm It's fine. Discussing Mari in terms of how she's similar to/different from other characters is valid-you just can't make that the end-all of her as a character, because you're just reducing her to a set of qualities she does(n't) share with other characters, instead of viewing her as her own character.

[]: Re: (Rei + Asuka) - Tragedy = Mari?             Quote Post by Blockio » Mon Jun 07, 2021 6:34 pm Exactly that, yeah. Discussing character differences and similarities is good and important, but "if we take x character, remove a core element and add something that wasn't there before, we get a different character" is above everything else just kind of a non-statement; even the times when it might not be wrong, there is no sense or wisdom to it
